assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 35-43 detail each criterion individually: (1) 'Single license filter (PASS)' with validate_license_param and WHERE license IN clause, (2) 'Comma-separated license filter (PASS)' with comma splitting and is_in clause, (3) 'Invalid license returns 400 (PASS)' with spdx::Expression::parse rejection, (4) 'Pagination integration (PASS)' with filter applied before count/pagination, (5) 'Response shape unchanged (PASS)' with unchanged return type. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' for these same five criteria.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in the diff match the task specification exactly: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created). No out-of-scope files touched.' Lines 19 elaborate: 'The diff modifies exactly the files listed in the task\'s "Files to Modify" section (list.rs, service/mod.rs) and creates exactly the file listed in "Files to Create" (tests/api/package.rs). No additional files are touched.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No mention anywhere in the outputs of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The report describes reading and verifying code (e.g., 'The validate_license_param function parses the license parameter', 'Test test_list_packages_single_license_filter seeds three packages') but never mentions editing, writing, or modifying any source code files. All output files are verification/analysis documents.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md ends with 'Overall: PASS' on line 56 and presents a structured table of check results. There is no mention of merging, approving, or auto-merging the PR anywhere in any output file. The report presents verdicts and evidence for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, private keys, secrets, or hardcoded credentials found in the diff. The only string literals are SPDX license identifiers (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only, INVALID-999) and error messages.' Lines 27-28 elaborate: 'The diff contains no secrets, credentials, API keys, or private key material. String literals in the diff are limited to SPDX license identifiers used in test data, URL paths for API endpoints, and error message templates.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/package.rs is a newly created file with 80 lines of test code. No existing tests were modified or removed.' Lines 53-54 elaborate: 'Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE): tests/api/package.rs is an entirely new file. No existing test files were modified or removed. All 4 tests are net-new additions covering the new license filter functionality.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains all four domain sections: 'Intent Alignment' (line 17) with Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), and Commit Traceability (PASS); 'Security' (line 25) with Sensitive Patterns (PASS); 'Correctness' (line 29) with CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), and Verification Commands (N/A); 'Style/Conventions' (line 47) with Test Quality (PASS) combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality (N/A), plus Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). The summary table on lines 5-15 also lists all these verdicts.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 19-23): file-by-file comparison naming list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs with modify/create status and line count (~30 implementation + ~80 tests). Security (lines 27-28): specific pattern scanning noting 'String literals in the diff are limited to SPDX license identifiers used in test data, URL paths for API endpoints, and error message templates. The SPDX validation uses the spdx crate\'s Expression::parse method.' Correctness (lines 33-45): per-criterion code-level verification with function names (validate_license_param), SQL clause details (WHERE license IN), test names (test_list_packages_single_license_filter), and specific assertions. Style/Conventions (lines 49-54): detailed test quality assessment noting 4 distinct scenarios, doc comment style (///), Given/When/Then pattern, and no copy-paste patterns.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | ... Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 52: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR.' Test Quality is marked PASS (not degraded by Eval Quality), confirming Eval Quality does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 52: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR.'). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. The only sub-task related mention is line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created', confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain analysis sections (Intent Alignment starts at line 17, Security at line 25, Correctness at line 29, Style/Conventions at line 47), indicating they are determined independently. The reasons match: Review Feedback is N/A due to no review comments, and Root-Cause Investigation is N/A due to no sub-tasks created.
